title: RAG is dead, right?? — Kuba Rogut, Turbopuffer
summary: Kuba Rogut argues that RAG is not dead but rather misunderstood. The real problem was a narrow definition of retrieval, which should encompass vector search, full text search, glob, regex, and filters used iteratively. Cursor's measured gains from semantic search and rising Google search volume contradict the "RAG is dead" narrative.
writeup: Kuba Rogut of Turbopuffer pushes back on the "RAG is dead" claim, arguing the technique was misunderstood rather than obsolete. The core fix is a broader definition of retrieval: not just vector search but also full text search, glob, regex, and filters applied iteratively. He cites Cursor's measured gains from semantic search and rising Google search volume as evidence against the narrative. The talk also situates retrieval alongside concepts including text embeddings, test-time compute, and cached compute.
